exemplar.title:
To what extent has Amazon's enforcement of the cost leadership
strategy enabled it to remain the number one e-commerce company in recent years?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent has Amazon's enforcement of the cost leadership
strategy enabled it to remain the number one e-commerce company in recent years?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Explicit selection of âchangeâ as the key concept linked to Amazonâs logistics, product range and technology initiatives
Consistent references to Amazonâs operational data (regionalisation, warehouse automation, AI) to illustrate strategic change
weaknesses: No clear definition or theoretical framing of âchangeâ to ground the analysis
Discussion often drifts into descriptive revenue figures without tying them back to the key concept
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student shows a good understanding of âchangeâ and makes clear analytical connections to Amazon, the lack of a formal definition and uneven integration across sections limits the depth of analysis. This merits a 2/5 to reflect moderate but not sustained integration of the key concept.

strengths: Provides exactly four supporting documents, meeting the 3â5 document requirement
Includes relevant sources such as the annual report and an academic journal article
weaknesses: No evaluation of source credibility, bias or limitations
Data tables (e.g., Yahoo Finance ratios) are not interpreted or integrated into the discussion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the quantity and basic relevance of documents are adequate, the absence of source evaluation and minimal interpretation of presented data limit the depth and critical use of supporting materials. A 1/4 reflects this superficial engagement.

strengths: Selection of Ansoff Matrix, STEEPLE and ratio analysis aligns with the research question
Tools are generally applied to Amazonâs strategies (product moves, external factors, financial metrics)
weaknesses: Applications are descriptive rather than analytical (e.g., generic Ansoff discussion)
Key STEEPLE dimensions (economic, political, ethical) are omitted, limiting breadth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student makes an adequate selection of relevant tools but the execution remains largely descriptive and narrow in scope. Assigning 2/4 captures the acceptable but under-developed application.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Use of recent revenue, margin and ROCE figures demonstrates engagement with quantitative data
Some analysis links AWS growth to profit margins and discusses market development risk
weaknesses: Data set is modest and empirical evidence is inconsistently integrated
Analysis sometimes lacks benchmarking and deeper evaluation (e.g., missing industry comparators)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Analysis shows some effort to interpret data and evaluate the research question, but the uneven depth and modest data selection limit its effectiveness. A 2/5 reflects this partial but incomplete evaluation.

strengths: Conclusions summarise strategic findings and are consistent with presented evidence
Research question is explicitly restated and addressed in conclusion
weaknesses: Does not quantify âextentâ of cost leadership success with a closing definitive statement
Lacks a strong final evaluative judgment on Amazonâs overall strategic performance
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusions are coherent and evidence-based, directly answering the research question, but fall short of fully articulating the extent of cost leadership success. A 2/3 reflects solid consistency with room for a more decisive evaluative statement.

strengths: Clear table of contents and section headings provide navigability
Logical sequence from methodology through analysis enhances flow
weaknesses: Inconsistent sub-heading hierarchy and minor formatting glitches
TOC lacks page numbers, reducing usability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Organization is generally appropriate but undermined by formatting inconsistencies and missing page references. A score of 1/2 reflects adequate structure with notable lapses.

strengths: Includes all required cover details, word count, references and appendices
Footnotes and appendices are present, demonstrating awareness of presentation requirements
weaknesses: Omission of page numbers in contents impedes navigation
Appendix figures are not integrated into the main text narrative
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All required elements are present, but missing page references and weak integration of footnoted materials limit overall presentation quality. A 1/2 appropriately captures this level.

strandSelections.reasoning: A logical structure is evidentâtitle page, contents, section headings and appendicesâallowing the reader to follow the argument.  Minor lapses (uneven sub-heading hierarchy, occasional formatting glitches) prevent a perfect score but overall organization is âappropriate throughout,â warranting band 2.

The documents are relevant: the annual report supplies primary financial data, Yahoo Finance offers competitor ratios, and the journal article underpins theoretical claims.  Depth is adequateâtables, graphs and extracts are includedâbut evaluation of source limitations is minimal and some data (e.g., formula-filled Yahoo table) is not interpreted, so the work fits ârelevant and sufficiently in-depthâ rather than the top band which expects a wide range and richer commentary.

The IA includes the required cover details, table of contents, word count, references, and appendices.  Hence all required elements are present, fulfilling the highest band for this checklist-style criterion.
Exactly four supporting documents are provided and referenced (Annual Report, FourWeekMBA, Yahoo Finance, academic journal).  This satisfies the 3â5 range specified for the middle bands.

Application is generally sound: the Ansoff discussion maps Amazonâs product moves, STEEPLE links social/tech factors to cost, and ratios are interpreted against competitors.  Yet some applications are superficial (e.g., STEEPLE limited to two factors; ratios lack detailed benchmarking), so relevance is clear but execution only âadequate,â consistent with band 3.

The student selects recent revenue, margin and ROCE figures plus delivery-speed data; these are appropriate but the data set is modest.  Usage is decent (e.g., ROCE calculated in appendix), matching the rubric phrase âadequate selection and use,â hence band 3.

Analysis extends beyond descriptionâlinking AWS growth to profit margins and discussing risk within market developmentâbut the depth varies and integration with the key concept is intermittent.  This aligns with âsome analysis of the research question,â appropriate to band 3.

The research question is restated and explicitly answered (âAmazon enables their cost leadership strategy to a big extentâ¦â).  The response directly addresses the âextentâ element, fitting band 3 which requires an explicit answer.

Three standard BM toolsâthe Ansoff Matrix, STEEPLE and ratio analysisâare appropriate for the research question and cover growth, external environment and financial performance.  This constitutes an adequate, though not especially expansive, selection.

The conclusion summarises findings for each strategic path and is consistent with the evidence presented (e.g., acknowledges cost pressures alongside revenue growth).  No contradictions with data are observed, placing it in the top band for this strand.

Connections to Amazon are consistently present: financial metrics, operational initiatives, and specific services such as AWS and Prime are analysed.  The student links each Ansoff, STEEPLE or ratio point back to Amazonâs cost leadership.  Nonetheless, integration is unevenâsome tool explanations remain generic and certain assertions (e.g., âthird-party sellers reinforce cost leadershipâ) are not fully analysed.  This represents clear analysis with partial rather than pervasive integration, aligning with band 3.

The student explicitly selects âchangeâ as the key concept and repeatedly links Amazonâs adjustments in logistics, product range and technology to that concept.  References to regionalisation, warehouse automation and new AI services illustrate how strategic change supports cost leadership.  However, the discussion drifts into descriptive commentary (e.g., lengthy revenue figures) that is only loosely tied back to the concept, so integration is clear but not sustained throughout the whole IA, fitting the band 3 descriptor of âgood understanding with clear analytical thinkingâ rather than the higher bands that require full integration.

annotations.comment: Consider adding a brief definition of âchangeâ with citation to IB syllabus or academic source, then explain its relevance to Amazonâs strategy.
The table of contents provides a clear organizational framework, enhancing navigability.
The contents list omits page references; without page numbers, readers may struggle to locate sections efficiently.
The research question is clearly stated, and the presence of the key concept on the title page demonstrates initial organizational coherence.
Although âChangeâ is named as the key concept, there is no definition or theoretical framing; the student should articulate what âchangeâ entails in a business context.
The introduction provides useful background but does not connect Amazonâs evolution to the concept of change, missing an analytical link.
Methodology heading clearly delineates research design and sources, aiding structural clarity.
The list of supporting documents is introduced, but lacks comment on each sourceâs credibility or limitations.
The bullet list covers four supporting documents, demonstrating adequate breadth for 3â5 required range.
Include page numbers beside each TOC entry to improve document usability and adhere to presentation standards.
Descriptions of each document do not assess source authority, peer-review status, or potential bias.
Integrate a brief evaluation of each supporting documentâs reliability and relevance to strengthen depth.
The Ansoff Matrix is appropriately selected but its application remains largely descriptive without analytical insight.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The market penetration subsection clearly outlines pricing and Prime benefits to build customer loyalty.
Incorporate quantitative performance data (e.g., revenue lift, penetration rates) to demonstrate the cost-leadership impact of each Ansoff strategy.
Diversification mentions AWS profitability but omits comparisonâanalysis should contrast new services with core operations.
Market development risks are identified in text but lack empirical evidence or case examples to validate claims.
Support risk discussion with specific figuresâe.g., cost overrun amounts, market expansion ROIâto substantiate evaluation.
Social factor discussion of âClimate Pledge Friendlyâ initiative effectively links environmental change to cost leadership.
Extend STEEPLE to include at least economic and political factorsâe.g., inflation impact on input costs, regulatory changes.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Gross profit ratio is cited but the calculation steps and comparators with industry benchmarks are not shown.
STEEPLE analysis covers only social and technological factors; a full STEEPLE requires political, economic, legal, environmental, and ethical dimensions.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The opening of the conclusion section acknowledges challenges but does not tie them back to the core research question.
The conclusions effectively summarise Ansoff findings for each strategic path, maintaining consistency with evidence.
The ROCE formula is correctly displayed in LaTeX, enhancing clarity of the financial measure.
Clarify why Apple and Walmart were chosen as benchmarks; discuss industry context and relevance of these comparators.
Revenue growth metrics are reported but lack analysis of how these changes reflect Amazonâs strategic adaptations under the âchangeâ concept.
Relate year-over-year revenue changes explicitly to strategic shiftsâe.g., new services or automation investments driving growth.
Footnotes reference appendices but the narrative does not interpret or integrate these figures into the discussion.
Add a closing sentence that explicitly states to what extent cost leadership has enabled Amazon to remain #1, answering the research question.
